macro_read: Macro classifier reads NEUTRAL (score 0.55) with growth AND inflation both accelerating, rates neutral, credit stable — a risk-ON-but-fearful tape (CNN F&G 25) sitting on a hawkish-Fed/no-2026-cuts pivot where no strategist prices multiple expansion, so the +30-50%/12mo goal is reached not through demand-cycle beta but through DATED structural-special-sit catalysts (forced-sellers/spins #1 per the regime doc) and balance-sheet/carry anchors, with the single high-beta cyclical reach sized down.

apex_basket.thesis: Over-levered power-and-infrastructure holdco whose thin equity is a defined-risk call on the dated Long Ridge/MARA sale that mechanically removes ~$1.16B of plant debt and re-rates the stub to its sum-of-parts.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Forced-realization / asset-sale special-sit — the regime's #1 lane (event carries the thesis, orthogonal to the demand cycle and to rates).
apex_basket.entry_posture: on_confirmation: Long Ridge/MARA sale clearance & close by Q3 2026 (contractual backstop Nov 30 2026)
apex_basket.goal_note: A dated Q3-2026 closing removes the leverage overhang and re-rates the equity ~+40% to a verified-floor-backed SoP; defined-risk to the recently-tested ~$3.9 floor.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: New conviction-5/verdict-A catalyst special-sit not on the prior ledger; the dated asset sale with a verified floor is exactly the asymmetric event the balanced stance wants.

apex_basket.thesis: Blended-multiple government-services + sustainable-tech contractor that the street values as one company near a 52-week low, while the Jan 4 2027 separation forces each half onto its own peer group and unlocks the discount.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Dated spinoff — the regime's #2 lane; defense/gov-services revenue is recession-defensive and the break-up is the re-rate, not the cycle.
apex_basket.entry_posture: on_confirmation: KBR separation into two listed companies, January 4 2027
apex_basket.goal_note: Hard Jan-4-2027 separation date with essentially no downside to the ~$33.2 verified trough floor and ~+47% to base-case SoP — a dated re-rate inside the window.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: New catalyst special-sit; a hard-dated spin with a floor at the current price is a near-costless asymmetric seat the ledger does not hold.

apex_basket.thesis: A telecom conglomerate priced as a slowly-melting holdco while the dated H2-2027 Ziggo spin (plus the ~July-2026 Vodafone-JV cash) converts the holdco discount into distributed shares and cash.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Spinoff / sum-of-parts realization — regime lane #2; the break-up is the driver, not linear-media trends.
apex_basket.goal_note: ~+64% to SoP as the committed Ziggo spin and Vodafone-JV cash close the discount; near-term ~July-2026 cash inflow plus a larger $30+ bull tail if a later UK separation lands.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: New catalyst special-sit, defined-risk to the ~$9.5 floor; a committed dated break-up the ledger does not hold.

apex_basket.thesis: EPS-compounding hospital operator at ~6x earnings near a 52-week low where the market conflates a dated, partial (~12-13%, phased from 2028) Medicaid DPP haircut with terminal collapse while acute margins expand and the buyback shrinks the float.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Recession-defensive cash compounder — the carry/balance-sheet anchor the balanced stance wants; widest risk-reward to SoP in the value sleeve.

wheel.rationale: Selling the ~$135 put pays you to wait at a 52-week-low support level on a self-funding compounder you are happy to own at a discount.

apex_basket.goal_note: ~+63% to SoP as the market re-rates a +150bp acute-margin trajectory and float shrink once the DPP haircut is recognized as dated/partial, not terminal.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: Held since 2026-06-21; thesis intact (conviction 3, verdict B), widest value upside, and a defensive anchor for the de-gross scenario — no strictly-better orthogonal swap.

apex_basket.thesis: Net-cash, beat-every-quarter IT-services cash machine at ~7.4x FCF and a 52-week low, on the WINNING side of the AI-efficiency curve (rev/employee rising, margin up on fewer heads) while the labor-arbitrage cohort de-rates together.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Balance-sheet/FCF anchor on the right side of an AI secular theme — carry that pays while you wait for the re-rate.

wheel.rationale: Selling the ~$35 put pays you to wait at the 52-week low on a net-cash franchise you would happily own cheaper.

apex_basket.goal_note: ~+42% to SoP as the market separates the AI-efficiency winner from the eroding arbitrage shops; net cash + buyback fund the wait.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: Held since 2026-06-21; thesis intact and it is the durable (winning-side) half of the labor-arbitrage theme, so it carries the theme at full weight while BAH is demoted to runner-up.

apex_basket.thesis: A 30-year three-segment payments operator at ~7.8x FCF where the EFT infrastructure leg (regulator-mandated ATM-as-a-Service + Ren processing) deserves a payments-software multiple the market denies the whole, with a live Voss activist process as the soft catalyst.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Activist-feeder special-sit with deep FCF support; the regime's #4 activist lane sourcing a potential separation, downside-protected by carry.
apex_basket.goal_note: ~+45% to SoP if the Voss-prompted board process surfaces the infrastructure leg's true multiple; FCF yield underpins the wait.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: Held since 2026-06-21; thesis intact and it carries the payments-disintermediation theme at full weight, so WEX (same theme, FIRED catalyst) is dropped to a runner-up to respect the 2-name theme cap.

apex_basket.thesis: Wide-moat, fortress-balance-sheet (about 29% of cap in net cash) games compounder at a China-ADR fear-print of ~12x EV/FCF on a 64%/30%-margin franchise, with globalization (Marvel Rivals, Where Winds Meet) an unpriced free option.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Fortress-balance-sheet defensive compounder uncorrelated to US/European cycle — the de-gross ballast of the book.
apex_basket.goal_note: Below the 12-month +30-50% bar (~+16% to base SoP), but the WIDE moat + ~29% net-cash + free global-franchise option make it the book's defensive ballast and a longer-horizon compounder worth its seat in a fearful tape.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: Held since 2026-06-14; thesis intact, no broken-thesis trigger, and it is the orthogonal China-uncorrelated anchor — kept on its de-gross-ballast role despite a sub-goal 12-month base case.

apex_basket.thesis: A disciplined infrastructure-software roll-up at ~6.4x FCF / ~81% gross margin that gushes cash (~$320M FY26 unlevered FCF) and steadily de-levers EV from debt to equity, a textbook Thoma-Bravo-LBO profile providing the downside.
apex_basket.regime_fit: High-FCF de-levering compounder with LBO-takeout optionality (fits the open M&A/PE-dry-powder window); carry-anchored.

wheel.rationale: Selling the ~$30 put pays you to wait on a cash-gushing de-levering software name with LBO floor support.

apex_basket.goal_note: ~+24% to SoP plus an LBO-takeout tail; below the strict +30% bar on the base case but the deep FCF yield + de-leveraging + takeout optionality earn the seat as a downside-protected carry leg.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: Held since 2026-06-21; thesis intact and it carries the ai-displacement theme at full weight (as the cash-harvest end), so FIS (same theme, FIRED) stays a runner-up — no theme breach.

apex_basket.thesis: A self-funded FCF compounder priced as a stale levered hospital holdco where the ~38-40%-margin USPI ambulatory franchise alone backstops most of the equity and the ACA-subsidy headwind is concentrated in the hospital half.
apex_basket.regime_fit: Defensive cash compounder; the ACA-subsidy catalyst has FIRED so re-rated to a downside-protected defensive anchor (not a conviction-4 special-sit), paired under the reimbursement-compression cap with UHS.

wheel.rationale: Selling the ~$170 put pays you to wait on a self-funding ambulatory franchise you are happy to own at a discount.

apex_basket.goal_note: ~+12% to SoP base — a defensive carry anchor, not a 12-month re-rate; held for de-gross protection and the USPI backstop, sized as an anchor given the FIRED catalyst.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: Held since 2026-06-21; thesis intact (USPI backstop) but the catalyst has FIRED, so re-graded to a defensive anchor and kept under the reimbursement-compression combined cap with UHS rather than dropped.

apex_basket.thesis: A deleveraging European tier-1 auto supplier whose thin equity is a levered call on the signed, dated Apollo/Interiors sale (EUR1.82B EV, at least EUR1B net-debt cut, closing end-2026) that mechanically de-levers to a re-rated SoP.
apex_basket.regime_fit: The book's single high-beta cyclical/European reach — carried for the dated Apollo de-lever catalyst but SIZED DOWN and entered on weakness given the European-cyclical-recession tail.
apex_basket.goal_note: ~+73% to SoP as the signed Apollo Interiors sale closes end-2026 and the at-least-EUR1B net-debt cut re-rates the thin equity; the dated catalyst, not the auto cycle, is the driver.
apex_basket.decision_rationale: Held since 2026-06-21; the dated Apollo sale (PENDING_HARD) is intact and is the widest-upside catalyst in the value sleeve — kept but flagged as the lone cyclical reach, sized down and entered on weakness.

runner_ups.note: Conviction-4 merger-arb special-sit (July 29 2026 outside date, host-country approvals) but only ~+21% to the deal-resolved target off the working live with a thin TINY_FLOOR cushion — held as the first alternate to any special-sit leg that breaks; would seat over FRVIA if the European cyclical tail worsens.
+37% to SoP and cheap, but the Impactive cooperation catalyst has FIRED and it would breach the 2-name payments-disintermediation cap alongside EEFT/NEXI — runner-up only.
+50% to SoP but Worldpay/TSYS catalyst FIRED and it would breach the ai-displacement cap alongside PRGS; quality gap (4% net margin) is real — runner-up.
+21% to SoP; demoted from a seat to keep labor-arbitrage-deflation at one full-weight name (CTSH, the winning side) rather than loading the melting theme.

+57% to SoP (cheapest payments name) but a 2026-06-14 drop with no documented thesis change since, and it would be the 3rd payments-disintermediation name — held as alternate behind EEFT, not re-added.
+49% to SoP energy E&P but catalyst FIRED (merger + South Texas sale closed) and adds commodity/oil beta the balanced stance is sizing down — runner-up.

director_memo:
RISK STANCE: balanced. Macro classifier is NEUTRAL (0.55) with growth AND inflation both accelerating, rates neutral, credit stable; the regime doc reads risk-ON-but-fearful (CNN F&G 25) on a hawkish-Fed/no-2026-cuts pivot where no strategist prices multiple expansion. That argues against full risk-on demand-cycle beta and FOR reaching the +30-50%/12mo goal through DATED structural-special-sit catalysts (the regime's forced-seller/spinoff #1-#2 lanes) and balance-sheet/carry anchors, with the lone cyclical reach (FRVIA) sized down and entered on weakness.

CONSTRUCTION: 3 equity_special_sit legs (FIP conv-82 asset sale; KBR conv-76 Jan-2027 spin; LBTYK conv-70 Ziggo spin) sized to their verified floors at <=1.5% risk-to-floor each; 7 value/quality anchors. AAUC (conv-4 arb) sits first alternate — its +21%/thin-floor is weaker asymmetry than the three seated. B13 SLEEVE: every binary_prob name (BBIO, EYPT, ZYME, IMCR, DBVT) is HARD-EXCLUDED per constraint; FUN (spread/activist) and PRX.AS (recovery, no dated milestone) were screened — FUN's milestone (2027-03-31) is outside the tighter window and PRX is undated, so neither is seated; the seated special-sits all come from the catalyst-framed opus_catalyst dossiers in results_regime.

CORRELATION/EXPOSURE STRESS (the proposed 10): (a) DEMAND-CYCLE beta — only FRVIA.PA is a true high-beta European cyclical; FIP/KBR/LBTYK are event-driven and the value anchors are defensive (hospitals, net-cash IT-services, fortress-balance-sheet games, infra software), so a European-cyclical-recession hits one sized-down name, not the book. (b) REGULATORY JURISDICTION — no sector_regulatory convergence cluster present (PLX.PA/Pluxee not seated); FRVIA/LBTYK carry idiosyncratic EU clearance, KBR/FIP US — no shared regulatory factor >3. (c) LIQUIDITY/POSITIONING — no small-cap de-gross cluster; the names are mostly liquid large/mid-caps. (d) POSTURE — only one wait_for_weakness (FRVIA) and three on_confirmation/scale_in event legs, so timing is not one correlated flush bet. (e) SECULAR-THEME — see below. The book PASSES the European-cyclical-recession + correlated-de-gross stress: NTES (China, WIDE) and the US event legs are the orthogonal ballast.

SECULAR-THEME CONCENTRATION: (1) reimbursement-compression = THC + UHS (2 names, at cap) — KEPT-WITH-CAP, combined_caps {THC,UHS} max_units 2; both NARROW so neither halves. (2) labor-arbitrage-deflation = CTSH only — RESOLVED by DIVERSIFICATION: BAH demoted to runner-up so the melting arbitrage theme carries one full-weight name (CTSH, the AI-efficiency WINNER). (3) payments-disintermediation = EEFT only at a seat — RESOLVED by DIVERSIFICATION: WEX and NEXI.MI dropped to runner-ups so the theme carries one seated name. (4) ai-displacement = PRGS only — RESOLVED: FIS demoted to runner-up. (5) energy-transition-loser = FRVIA only; linear-media-decline = LBTYK only. No theme exceeds 2; no single melting tail (e.g. AI-displacement) carries the book.

ROTATION DISCIPLINE vs the ledger: KEEP all 7 currently-held names whose theses are intact (FRVIA, THC, NTES, CTSH, EEFT, PRGS, UHS). DROP NSP — held since 2026-06-21 but now trades ~$42.6 ABOVE its ~$40 base SoP, the trough-earnings recovery thesis is realized (no upside left), a clean realized exit not a broken thesis. DROP SCR.PA and AD.AS — both fell to conviction-2/verdict-C this run (below the eligibility bar), thesis no longer carries a seat. ADD FIP/KBR/LBTYK as new dated catalyst special-sits. No RE-ADDs: NEXI.MI (dropped 2026-06-14) graded well (+57%) but a better grade is NOT a documented thesis change and it would breach the payments cap, so it stays a runner-up.

BEAR REBUTTAL (one per apex seat, written before final sizing): FIP — if the Long Ridge/MARA sale slips past the Nov-30 backstop or clears at a haircut, the equity is still a money-losing, negative-book-equity holdco at 9.75% debt cost and the floor is soft. KBR — a politically-driven gov-services budget cut or a separation delay leaves a blended ~10x contractor with the spin un-fired and no re-rate. LBTYK — the holdco discount is perennial and the Ziggo spin can be pushed/structured to leak value, so the gap may simply persist as it has for years. UHS — the Medicaid DPP haircut could prove larger and earlier than the dated/partial read, turning the cheap multiple into a justified one. CTSH — gen-AI may compress IT-services pricing faster than the rev/employee gains, making CTSH a melting-arbitrage shop in disguise rather than the winner. EEFT — stablecoin/wallet rails arbitrage the cross-border take-rate toward zero and the Voss process fizzles, leaving a structurally-shrinking ATM/remittance book. NTES — a single weak game launch or a China-ADR regulatory shock de-rates the whole franchise and the global option never monetizes. PRGS — the AI-displacement of mid-market software erodes the roll-up's organic base while the debt stays, and no LBO bid arrives. THC — the catalyst has already FIRED so there is no near-term re-rate driver, and a deeper ACA-subsidy hit pressures the hospital half faster than USPI can backstop. FRVIA.PA — a European auto recession craters volumes before the Apollo sale closes, and a levered, ERODING-moat tier-1 supplier's thin equity is the first thing to gap down in a de-gross.
